title: Woody’s Grill & Tap in Fenway will permanently close
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Woody's Grill & Tap on Hemenway Street will close permanently after serving final customers Thursday night.
The Fenway restaurant, known for wood-fired pizza, is near Fenway Park and Symphony Hall.
Woody's has been a local favorite, hosting art exhibits and poetry readings for the community.

title: A royal family dined at a popular Somerville restaurant this week
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Belgium's royal family dined at Sarma Restaurant in Somerville to celebrate Princess Elisabeth's Harvard graduation.
Sarma, known for its Mediterranean cuisine, is a popular dining spot with a waitlist numbering in the thousands.
King Philippe, Queen Mathilde, and Princess Elisabeth were reportedly seen at the restaurant by local Reddit users.

title: What to know about the rejection of Healey’s Parole Board nominee as backlash builds
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Governor's Council rejected Vincent DeMore's Parole Board nomination, despite support from Gov. Maura Healey and district attorneys.
DeMore, a former Suffolk County prosecutor, faced opposition for his prosecutorial background, with over 400 signatures urging his rejection.
Rejection follows scrutiny of Parole Board after parolee-involved Cambridge shooting, highlighting concerns about parole policies and public safety.

title: Mike Vrabel talks offseason distractions, potential trades, and more
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel addressed the media about offseason challenges, including personal controversies and leadership, as team activities commence.
Vrabel mentioned uncertainty about potential trades, including speculation on acquiring Eagles’ All-Pro wideout A.J. Brown.
New England's 2026 schedule is demanding, with six nationally televised games and a Week 1 matchup against Super Bowl champions Seattle.

title: Video shows Sullivan’s Tap patrons running for cover when shots fired near TD Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Police reported gunshots near TD Garden Tuesday at 10:45 p.m., causing patrons at Sullivan's Tap to seek cover.
The shooting shattered the front door of Hotel Indigo on Friend Street and caused ballistic damage to a parked vehicle.
Police are investigating and have recovered ballistic evidence; no injuries reported and no arrests made.

title: Wu cancels Harvard speaking engagement rather than cross grad-student picket line
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu canceled her Harvard Law speaking engagement to avoid crossing a graduate student picket line.
The union has been on strike since April 21 after 15 months of contract negotiations with Harvard University stalled.
Wu's offer to participate virtually or acknowledge the strike at the podium was declined by the union.

title: Mike’s Pastry is coming to Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Mike's Pastry will open in Logan Airport's Terminal C, offering filled-to-order cannoli and other bakery items.
The new location will feature individual cheesecake slices, lobster tails, and a full espresso bar.
Operating hours will be 5 a.m. to 10 p.m. daily, enhancing passenger experience with local Boston flavors.

title: ‘Stranger Things’ actor Joe Keery joins star-studded cast for Broadway debut
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Joe Keery and Julia Louis-Dreyfus will debut on Broadway in 'Other Desert Cities' with a star-studded cast.
'Other Desert Cities' revival features Ed Harris, Allison Janney, and Lily Rabe and starts previews on September 29, 2026.
The play, about a family's hidden scandals, will run for 16 weeks at Broadway's Hudson Theatre until January 17, 2027.

title: Police close South Boston beach after ‘crowds of youth’ break into fights
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Carson Beach parking lot in South Boston was closed after fights erupted among crowds of youth.
State police dispersed groups from Carson Beach and Castle Island due to fights, loud music, and alcohol use.
No injuries or arrests were reported; the parking lot and part of Day Boulevard were closed but later reopened.

title: Clover says it’s closing for good
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab is closing all 11 restaurants by May 28, citing inability to find a buyer.
The vegetarian chain faced financial difficulties, including inflation and competition from other healthy lifestyle chains.
Clover started as a food truck in 2008 and employed 240 people post-bankruptcy in 2024.
